title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL connection close failure can fail an already committed write
summary: Oracle MySQL connection close failures after commit could be surfaced as write failures.
observed: A committed batch could still raise because connection cleanup failed after the durable boundary was crossed.
expected: Cleanup failure after successful commit should be logged and must not fail the committed write.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for connection close after commit.
impact: A cleanup-only error could cause redelivery of data that was already committed.
delivery_semantics: The fix protects ACK-after-commit semantics by not treating post-commit cleanup as a failed write.
security: The cleanup warning avoids payload or secret logging while preserving operational visibility.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL connection close failures after commit are logged only.
Committed writes return success after the durable boundary is crossed.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and Oracle MySQL sink write tests.
documentation: No separate user documentation is required beyond release-note mention of the delivery semantics fix.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the cleanup fix is published.
